title: habenaria bracteosa
intro: Habenaria bracteosa, also known as the hairy orchid, is a species of orchid native to Sri Lanka, characterized by its unique hairy leaves and stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Habenaria bracteosa include its potential to treat various ailments, such as fever, headache, and digestive issues, as well as its use as a treatment for skin conditions and wounds. Herbal preparations of Habenaria bracteosa typically involve drying and grinding the plant's leaves and stems, which can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions for medicinal use.
